The key question: why does the diagnostics show "no session" after passwordless login?

Possibilities:
1. The passwordless login didn't actually work (backend error)
2. The session was installed but then the OWNER AUTO-LOGIN BLOCK in onAuthStateChange immediately signed it out because manualOwnerLoginRef was set to true AFTER handleSession, but onAuthStateChange fires during setSession BEFORE manualOwnerLoginRef is set to true. Let me look again at line 1704-1718:

```javascript
const { data: sessionData, error: sessionError } = await freshClient.auth.setSession({...});
if (sessionError) ...
const session = sessionData.session;
if (!session) ...
const challengeRequired = await requireTwoFactorIfNeeded(session, 'passwordless owner login');
...
const handled = await handleSession(session);
if (!handled.accepted) ...
// Mark manual owner login so onAuthStateChange allows this session.
manualOwnerLoginRef.current = true;
sessionInstalled = true;
```

The problem: `setSession` triggers `onAuthStateChange` immediately (synchronously or in a microtask), BEFORE `manualOwnerLoginRef.current = true` is set. The onAuthStateChange handler at line 1609 checks:
```javascript
if (isOwnerAdminEmail(session.user?.email) && !manualOwnerLoginRef.current) {
  ... sign out ...
}
```

So when `setSession` is called, `manualOwnerLoginRef.current` is still false, so the onAuthStateChange fires, sees an owner email, and immediately signs out! This is the bug! The session is installed and then immediately destroyed.

This is a critical bug. The fix is to set `manualOwnerLoginRef.current = true` BEFORE calling `setSession`.
